Gerontocracy: a state, society, or group governed by old people.

Hence, the correct answer is option A.

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Pick out the word in the following question that is different.

Faeces, foetus, stool, excreta.
questions.solution:
Here in the question, we have to find the word which is the odd one out among the given four.

As we all know faeces is the solid waste substance that people and animals get rid of from their body by passing it through the anus. Stool and faeces are same it is made of what is left after your digestive system absorbs nutrients and fluids from what you eat and drink. Excreta and faeces are the same words it also means human waste, but a foetus is an unborn animal or human being in its later stages of development. Hence, the foetus is the right answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

Arsenal: a collection of weapons and military equipment.

Asylum: shelter or protection from danger.

Hence, the correct answer is option A.

questions.q: The following question has four items. Select the item that does not belong to the group.
questions.solution:
Here in the question, we have to find the word which is the odd one out among the given four.

Here, cleavage means to split or to separate, fracture means to a break in bone or cartilage, severance means the ending of a connection or a relationship. As we can see that three word means to break something and have relatable meaning but divulge means give information about something or to make something public. Hence, divulge is the odd one.

Here in the question, we have to find the word which is the odd one out among the given four.
Here, pulverisation means crunch or the act of crushing, detrition is also grinding process, trituration means the process of grinding something to a fine powder. Whereas purgation means the act of purifying or cleansing. Hence, purgation is the odd one because it has a different meaning.

But laconic means to speak or write in a way that uses only a few words.

Hence, laconic has a different meaning from others so, it is the odd one.

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.solution:
Kindly refer to the video to get to know the context the word is generally used in.

Exonerate (Verb) :

1. (of an official body) absolve (someone) from blame for a fault or wrongdoing.

Ex. "an inquiry exonerated those involved"
Ex. It's another matter that in the equally sensational TANSI land-deal case, the Supreme Court finally exonerated Jayalalithaa of any wrong-doing.
 
Synonyms: absolve, clear, acquit, declare innocent, find innocent, pronounce not guilty, discharge.

2. release someone from (a duty or obligation).

Ex. "Pope Clement V exonerated the king from his oath to the barons"

Synonyms: release, discharge, relieve, free, liberate.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.q: The following question has four items. Select the item that does not belong to the group.
questions.solution:
Here in the question, we have to find the word which is the odd one out among the given four.

Here, reprehend means to blame or strongly judge a person or action. Censure means to tell somebody, that he or she has done something wrong. Chide means to blame somebody because they have done or said something wrong. So these three words have the same meaning but sprawl means to be stretched or spread out in an unnatural or ungraceful manner. Hence, sprawl is the right answer because it has a different meaning from others.

questions.q: In the following question, select the odd word from the given alternatives.
questions.solution:
The four options are:
- (a) Lemon: A fruit that grows above ground.
- (b) Carrot: A root vegetable that grows underground.
- (c) Beetroot: A root vegetable (specifically a taproot) that grows underground.
- (d) Turnip: A root vegetable (a type of taproot) that grows underground.
Carrot, Beetroot, and Turnip are all root vegetables that primarily grow beneath the soil. Lemon is a fruit that grows on a tree above the soil. Therefore, Lemon is the odd one out based on its botanical classification and growing location.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: English
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.solution:
Anatomy – The study of the bodily structure of an organism.

Demography - the composition of a particular human population.

Chronobiology - The study of duration of life.

Genetics - the study of heredity and the variation of inherited characteristics.

Hence, the correct answer is option C.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
